title: List API pagination skips the first page of records
product_summary: Acme projects REST API (Node/Express): paginated project listing consumed by customer integrations

ground_truth.root_cause: paginate() in src/pagination.js computes the slice offset as page * pageSize even though pages are 1-indexed per the documented API contract, so page 1 starts at the (pageSize+1)-th record. The first pageSize items are unreachable on any page and consumers see fewer distinct records than total reports.
ground_truth.fix_contract: With a 25-item collection and page_size=10, page 1 returns items 1-10, page 2 returns items 11-20 (together 20 unique items, no overlap, collection order preserved), and page 3 returns the remaining 5 with hasMore=false. Reported total, page echo, empty-collection handling, and query param clamping keep working.
expected_evidence: projects_listed events reporting returned_count=10 for page=1 but returned_count=5 for page=2 and 0 for page=3 against 25-item collections — only 15 records reachable
no change in traffic volume; the miscount is present on every request
distractors: suspected database ordering instability on createdAt ties
